page: Google Cloud Platform c3d-standard-90-lssd is a general-purpose instance powered by an AMD EPYC 9B14 processor utilizing the x86_64 architecture. It features 90 dedicated virtual CPUs, which correspond to 45 physical cores with hyper-threading, and 360 GB of system memory, yielding a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core. The instance includes 3216 GB of local NVMe SSD storage distributed across 8 local drives, but does not include any GPUs or complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

The AMD EPYC processor supports advanced instruction sets, including AVX-512 (AVX512F, AVX512_VNNI, and AVX512_BF16) and cryptographic acceleration (AES, VAES, and SHA_NI). In benchmark evaluations, the multi-core CPU performance ranks in the top tier, as demonstrated by stress-ng multi-core and PassMark CPU scores. Single-core performance is strong, placing in the top 25% of tested hardware. Software compilation with Clang and ray tracing workloads also achieve top-tier execution speeds.

Memory read bandwidth is highly efficient, reaching up to 6372.53 GB/sec for cached small blocks, though write bandwidth for large blocks remains average. The integrated NVMe SSD storage supports high-throughput database operations, with Redis SET operations achieving a top-tier rate of 117.73 million operations per second. Additionally, the system exhibits strong capabilities in static web serving and medium-scale LLM inference, though performance scales down to average levels when running large 70B parameter models.

Given its high resource density, the c3d-standard-90-lssd offers qualitative cost efficiency for workloads that require a balance of high-compute capacity and fast local storage without the overhead of dedicated GPU hardware. The combination of 90 vCPUs and over 3 TB of local NVMe SSD makes this instance highly suitable for large-scale database deployments, Redis caching layers, software compilation pipelines, and moderate machine learning inference tasks.
description: The Google Cloud Platform c3d-standard-90-lssd is a high-capacity general-purpose instance configured with 90 vCPUs and 360 GB of RAM, powered by an AMD EPYC 9B14 processor. It features 3216 GB of local NVMe SSD storage across 8 drives, offering high-speed local data access without GPU acceleration. The x86_64 architecture supports AVX-512 and cryptographic instructions, driving top-tier multi-core CPU performance and software compilation speeds. Benchmarks indicate strong memory read bandwidth and exceptional Redis throughput, peaking at 117.73 million operations per second. This resource profile provides qualitative cost efficiency for dense, non-GPU workloads. It is highly suited for large-scale database operations, Redis caching, software build pipelines, and medium-scale LLM inference.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform c3d-standard-90-lssd is an x86_64 instance with 90 AMD EPYC vCPUs, 360 GB RAM, and 3216 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, delivering top-tier multi-core CPU and database performance.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform c3d-standard-90-lssd features 90 AMD EPYC vCPUs, 360 GB RAM, and 3.2 TB of local NVMe SSD storage for high-performance workloads.
tagline: A high-core AMD EPYC instance featuring three terabytes of local NVMe SSD storage for demanding database and multi-threaded workloads.
bullet_points: 90 dedicated AMD EPYC vCPUs with 360 GB of RAM
3216 GB of high-speed local NVMe SSD storage
Top-tier multi-core CPU and software compilation performance
Excellent Redis throughput reaching 117.73 million operations per second
Optimized for large-scale databases, caching layers, and build pipelines
